sections.heading: Problem Statement
sections.content:
The detail panel is the primary place a developer inspects and acts on a node. Currently it renders 12 sections in a single scrolling column — badges, ID, location, tags, level_0, level_1, autodoc, level_2, test coverage, cortex steps, dFlow steps, and history. For a function with params, tests, steps, AND history, this is a long scroll with no way to jump to what you need.

Three specific problems:

**Too much in one scroll.** The panel mixes identity (ID, location, type), documentation (level_1, level_2), API structure (params, returns, raises), relationship data (test coverage, steps), and history (change log) into one linear dump. A developer looking for "why is this stale" has to scroll past docstrings and params to find the history section.

**No staleness actions.** The panel shows a staleness badge but provides no way to act on it — no verify button, no staleness cause explanation, no link to the node that triggered LINKED_STALE. The developer sees a colored badge and then has to switch to the CLI to do anything about it.

**Autodoc and docstring are interleaved confusingly.** The autodoc/API section (params, returns, raises) sits between level_1 and level_2. The workflow/dFlow steps are at the bottom, separated from the API info they logically belong with. And level_3_location (source path) is crammed into the Location field as a gray subtitle instead of being near the source code it points to.

**Solution: tabs.** Organize the panel into 5 tabs that group content by purpose — Overview (identity + actions), Docs (what the agent reads), API (structured interface + steps + source access), Relationships (how this node connects), and History (what changed).

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
**US-DP-01 — Navigate the panel without scrolling through everything**
As a developer, I want the detail panel organized into tabs (Overview, Docs, API, Relationships, History) so that I can jump directly to the information I need instead of scrolling past unrelated sections.

**US-DP-02 — See identity and take action immediately**
As a developer, I want the Overview tab to show the node's badges, ID, location, tags, one-liner summary (level_0), staleness cause explanation, and a verify button so that I can understand what the node is and act on its staleness in one place.

**US-DP-03 — Read docs as the agent sees them**
As a developer, I want the Docs tab to show level_1 (description) and level_2 (details) with explicit level labels so that I can see exactly what an agent reads at each detail level via `cortex_render`.

**US-DP-04 — See the full API interface and steps together**
As a developer, I want the API tab to show params/returns/raises (autodoc), the docstring, and workflow/dFlow steps (merged from both cortex level_steps and live dFlow API into a single coherent list) — with a "View Source" button to open the code panel — so that I get a complete picture of what the function takes, returns, does step-by-step, and can drill into the implementation.

**US-DP-05 — Understand why a node is stale**
As a developer, I want the Overview tab to show a staleness cause explanation — which hash changed (code, desc, both), which linked node triggered LINKED_STALE (with jump link), or which children caused composite inheritance — so that I know what action to take without cross-referencing the CLI.

**US-DP-06 — Verify a node directly from the panel**
As a developer, I want a "Mark Verified" button on the Overview tab (visible for stale nodes) with an optional reason field so that I can record my review without leaving the viz.

**US-DP-07 — See verification attribution on VERIFIED nodes**
As a developer, I want the Overview tab to show who verified a node (human or agent), when, and the reason so that I can judge whether the verification is still trustworthy.

**US-DP-08 — See which tests cover this node**
As a developer, I want the Relationships tab to show test coverage with pass/fail status and jump-to-test buttons so that I can see testing health alongside staleness without scrolling past docs.

**US-DP-09 — See composite children staleness breakdown**
As a developer, I want the Relationships tab to show a composite node's children with their individual staleness statuses so that I can understand inherited staleness without walking the composes tree manually.

**US-DP-10 — See inbound and outbound edges**
As a developer, I want the Relationships tab to list all edges (composes, documents, validates, calls, delegates_to) with clickable jump links to connected nodes so that I can navigate the graph from the detail panel.

**US-DP-11 — See change history and verification record**
As a developer, I want the History tab to show the change log (date, change_type, git SHA) and the verification record (who, when, reason) as separate sections so that I can trace the node's evolution and review history.

**US-DP-12 — See rename history**
As a developer, I want the History tab to show when a node was auto-renamed (old ID → new ID, date, file move) so that I can verify the scanner correctly matched a moved function.

**US-DP-13 — Open source code from the panel**
As a developer, I want a "View Source" button in the API tab that opens the code preview panel (bottom split) focused on this function's line range so that I can see the implementation after reviewing the API interface.

**US-DP-14 — View diff from history tab**
As a developer, I want clickable git SHA entries in the History tab's change log so that clicking a SHA opens the source panel in diff mode showing what changed at that specific commit — letting me trace exactly which code change triggered a staleness event.

**US-DP-15 — Diff toggle for stale nodes**
As a developer viewing a CONTENT_STALE node, I want a "Diff" toggle button in the source panel (next to Focus) that switches the Monaco editor to a side-by-side diff view — baseline at last verification vs current code — so I can see exactly what changed and decide whether docs need updating or the change was cosmetic.

sections.heading: Current Capabilities
sections.content:
| Capability | Status |
|---|---|
| Slide-out drawer on node selection | Done |
| Type badge + staleness badge + status display | Done |
| Node ID with copy button | Done |
| Location + level_3_location with copy button | Done |
| Tags as chips | Done |
| level_0 summary | Done |
| level_1 description (rendered as text) | Done |
| level_2 details (rendered as text) | Done |
| Autodoc/API section (params, returns, raises from dflow_meta) | Done |
| Test coverage table with pass/fail icons and jump buttons | Done |
| Cortex level_steps rendering (step_num, name, purpose, inputs, outputs, critical) | Done |
| Live dFlow workflow steps rendering (from API, with auto badges and jump buttons) | Done |
| Change history table (date, change_type, git SHA) | Done |
| Parallel data fetching (node, history, tests, steps, staleness-cause) | Done |
| Copy-to-clipboard on ID and location | Done |
| Tab-based layout (Overview, Docs, API, Relationships, History) | Done |
| Staleness cause explanation on Overview tab (server-side join) | Done |
| Verify button on Overview tab | Done |
| Verification attribution display (who, when, reason) | Done |
| Merged workflow steps (cortex level_steps + dFlow API in one list) | Done |
| "View Source" button in API tab (opens code preview panel) | Done (stub — panel coming Phase 3) |
| Composite children staleness breakdown on Relationships tab | Not started |
| Inbound/outbound edge list with jump links on Relationships tab | Done |
| Rename history on History tab | Done |
| Level labels on Docs tab (showing "Level 1", "Level 2" as agent sees them) | Done |
| Clickable git SHAs in History tab (open diff at that commit) | Not started |
| "Diff" toggle in source panel (Monaco diff editor, baseline vs current) | Not started |

**Tab specification:**

| Tab | Sections |
|---|---|
| **Overview** | Badges, ID + copy, location + copy, tags, level_0 summary, staleness cause, verify button, verification attribution |
| **Docs** | level_1 (labeled "Level 1 — Description"), level_2 (labeled "Level 2 — Details") |
| **API** | Params/returns/raises (autodoc), docstring, merged workflow steps, "View Source" button |
| **Relationships** | Test coverage, composite children breakdown, linked staleness causes with jump links, inbound/outbound edges |
| **History** | Change history timeline (with clickable SHAs), verification record, rename history |
